perturbation.original_text: We will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its financial advisor, J.P. Morgan & Co. Incorporated ("JPM"), or the Company's other representatives furnish to us in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by us, or by our representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or our review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). We recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential". The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us or our representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by us.
perturbation.changed_text: We will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its financial advisor, J.P. Morgan & Co. Incorporated ("JPM"), or the Company's other representatives furnish to us in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by us, or by our representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or our review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). We recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of some information provided, and the damage that could result to the Company if such information were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us or our representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by us.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence defining "Evaluation Materials," the scope of what constitutes confidential information becomes unclear. The clause stating 'The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential".' has been removed, creating an in-text contradiction because the agreement refers to "Evaluation Materials" but doesn't fully define it, creating ambiguity about what is protected under the confidentiality agreement and leaving uncertainty whether orally or in writing its considered part of the "Evaluation Materials."
perturbation.contradicted_text: We will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its financial advisor, J.P. Morgan & Co. Incorporated ("JPM"), or the Company's other representatives furnish to us in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by us, or by our representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or our review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). We recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of some information provided, and the damage that could result to the Company if such information were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us or our representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by us.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly defines 'Evaluation Materials' to include information furnished orally or in writing, regardless of whether it's specifically identified as 'confidential.' The changed text removes this explicit definition, creating ambiguity about what constitutes confidential information. The contradicted text assumes the existence of this clear definition, leading to a contradiction because the scope of 'Evaluation Materials' is no longer clearly defined, and it is unclear if information furnished orally or in writing is included. This uncertainty creates an in-text contradiction, making it harder to enforce the confidentiality agreement, as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between us and the Company (it being understood that our representatives shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between us and the Company and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase '(it being understood that our representatives shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially)' was removed from the original text. This removal creates an in-text contradiction because the first part of the sentence states Evaluation Materials must be kept confidential by representatives, yet the removed clause specifies the required understanding and agreement of representatives to confidentiality. Removing this creates ambiguity and the effectiveness of the initial confidentiality agreement becomes questionable, creating a contradiction because it is implied their confidentiality understanding isn't required to share.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between us and the Company and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither we nor our representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that representatives 'shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially.' This creates a clear expectation and obligation. The changed text removes this explicit requirement, creating ambiguity about the representatives' understanding and agreement to confidentiality. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which also assumes that representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials confidential. By removing the requirement for representatives to be informed and agree to confidentiality, the changed text weakens the confidentiality obligation outlined in the contradicted text. The removal implies that the representatives' confidentiality understanding isn't required to share, leading to a conflict with the overall confidential nature required, thereby, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, we agree that prior to the execution of a Sale Agreement with respect to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, we will not, and we will direct our representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between us and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof unless we are advised by counsel that we are required to make such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, we agree that prior to the execution of a Sale Agreement with respect to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, we will not disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between us and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof unless we are advised by counsel that we are required to make such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'and we will direct our representatives not to,' it creates an in-text contradiction. While the agreement still restricts the company from disclosing information, the absence of a similar directive for the representatives creates ambiguity and opens the door to potential leaks by representatives, weakening the confidentiality agreement and creating a contradiction within the overall section. The initial agreement is still valid, but not with the effect of the representatives disclosing the information, generating an effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, we agree that prior to the execution of a Sale Agreement with respect to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, we will not disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between us and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof unless we are advised by counsel that we are required to make such disclosure. The term "person" as used in this letter shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporation, the Company, governmental agency or body, stock exchange, partnership, association or individual.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a clause that 'we will direct our representatives not to disclose', which is removed in the changed text. The contradicted text aims to prevent the disclosure of information. By removing the directive to representatives, the changed text allows representatives to disclose information, directly contradicting the purpose and implied scope of the confidentiality agreement in the contradicted text. This creates a strong inconsistency within the document, because the changed text could reasonably allow representatives to disclose, while the contradicted text is still intending to prevent such disclosures.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of two years following the date hereof, you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or hire any officer, director, or employee of the Company or any of its subsidiaries or divisions with whom you have had contact or who became known to you in connection with your consideration of the Possible Transaction, except that you shall not be precluded from hiring any such employee who (i) initiates discussions regarding such employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by you, (ii) responds to any public advertisement placed by you, or (iii) has been terminated by the Company or its subsidiaries prior to commencement of employment discussions between you and such officer, director, or employee.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of two years following the date hereof, you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or hire any officer, director, or employee of the Company or any of its subsidiaries or divisions with whom you have had contact or who became known to you in connection with your consideration of the Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exceptions clause: 'except that you shall not be precluded from hiring any such employee who (i) initiates discussions regarding such employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by you, (ii) responds to any public advertisement placed by you, or (iii) has been terminated by the Company or its subsidiaries prior to commencement of employment discussions between you and such officer, director, or employee.' it creates an in-text contradiction. The original text specifies certain circumstances where hiring is permissible, but removing this creates an absolute prohibition on hiring. The initial text mentions that for 2 years you can't hire anyone. But later, it has 3 exceptions for hiring, and without that, it is a direct contradiction of the original statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of two years following the date hereof, you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or hire any officer, director, or employee of the Company or any of its subsidiaries or divisions with whom you have had contact or who became known to you in connection with your consideration of the Possible Transaction. We agree that until two years from the date of this Agreement, we will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained exceptions to the non-solicitation clause, allowing for the hiring of employees who initiated discussions, responded to public advertisements, or had been terminated by the company. The changed text removes these exceptions, creating an absolute prohibition on hiring. This directly contradicts the original agreement's intention and creates a conflicting obligation with the text mentioned in the 'Contradicted Text' section, since the modified text makes it impossible to execute those exceptions.
